tagline: The product development system for teams and agents
subheadline: Purpose-built for planning and building products. Designed for the AI era.
value_proposition: Linear is an AI-native product development platform that replaces fragmented issue tracking with an integrated system for planning, building, and shipping software — shared by humans and AI agents.
target_audience: Engineering and product teams at modern software companies, particularly those adopting AI-assisted development workflows and seeking to replace legacy tools like Jira.

testimonials: You just have to use it and you will see, you will just feel it. — Gabriel Peal, OpenAI
Our speed is intense and Linear helps us be action biased. — Nik Koblov, Ramp
Linear is excellent, just excellent. It has the right opinions for fast moving teams. — Kaz Nejatian, Opendoor

pricing.model: contact-sales with self-serve implied (Sign up CTA present)
pricing.price_anchoring: No pricing shown on homepage; 'Contact sales' alongside 'Get started' implies enterprise upsell; Startups segment mentioned in footer

features: AI-powered issue intake: converts Slack conversations and customer feedback into triaged, labeled, prioritized issues automatically
Product planning with initiatives, roadmaps, and PRDs aligned across teams
AI agent integration: delegate issues end-to-end to agents like GitHub Copilot, Codex, Cursor
Structural code diffs for reviewing human and agent output without leaving Linear
Progress monitoring via Pulse updates, cycle analytics, and dashboards
differentiators: 'Issue tracking is dead' — bold category repositioning away from Jira-era tools
'A new species of product tool' — claims a fundamentally new category
AI agents as first-class collaborators, not bolted-on features
Structural diffs for agent PR review built natively into the product
'Designed for the AI era' — positions competitors as legacy
trust_signals: Named enterprise customers: OpenAI, Ramp, Opendoor
Named testimonial givers with titles and companies
Changelog with dated releases signals active development
Enterprise and Startups tiers mentioned in footer

design_patterns.layout: Linear vertical scroll with named sections (Intake, Plan, Build, Diffs, Monitor), each pairing a bold headline with an animated product UI demo
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark-first with high-contrast white text and subtle gray tones; code blocks and UI panels use dark backgrounds
design_patterns.typography: Clean, modern sans-serif; large display headlines with tight tracking; monospace for code snippets
design_patterns.visual_style: Minimal and technical with a premium, opinionated aesthetic — closer to developer tooling than typical SaaS
notable_patterns: Live-looking product screenshots with animated agent activity (typewriter-style Bash commands, progress indicators)
Split-pane code diff view showing before/after for agent PR output
Numbered section flow (1.0 Intake → 2.0 Plan → 3.0 Build → 4.0 Diffs → 5.0 Monitor) framing the product as a complete system
Slack-style conversation thread embedded in page to show real-world intake workflow
Changelog section with specific dated releases to signal velocity

emotional_tone: Confident and aspirational with technical credibility — provocative ('Issue tracking is dead') but grounded in concrete product detail
key_objections_addressed: Is AI actually useful here or just a gimmick? — Addressed via specific agent demos (GitHub Copilot, Codex) doing real tasks
Will this work for my existing stack? — Addressed via named integrations (GitHub, Slack, MCP, Microsoft Teams)
Is it just for small teams? — Addressed via enterprise customer logos and 25,000 teams metric
Can I trust agent output? — Addressed via native diff review for agent PRs
missing_or_weak: No pricing transparency on the homepage — friction for self-serve evaluation
No free trial or freemium callout visible in hero — unclear entry point for new users
Security and compliance signals absent (no SOC 2, GDPR, SSO badges)
No comparison to Jira/Asana/Linear competitors explicitly, despite the 'issue tracking is dead' claim
Testimonials lack specificity — no before/after metrics or outcome data
No video or interactive demo — heavy reliance on static/animated screenshots
Footer mentions Startups tier but no explanation of what it includes
standout_elements: 'Issue tracking is dead' opens the page as a provocation — immediately signals category disruption
Numbered product narrative (1.0–5.0) frames the tool as a complete system, not a point solution
Showing actual agent CLI output (Bash commands, GitHub Copilot status) makes AI integration tangible and credible
Structural diff section is genuinely differentiated — no competitor shows this natively
Weekly Pulse demo with real-sounding project names and risks grounds the monitoring story in PM reality
Changelog embedded on homepage signals fast iteration and transparency
